transcript_text: Patient reports shoulder pain 7 out of 10. Starting manual therapy and therapeutic exercise.

latest_analysis.summary: Conversation segment reviewed: Patient reports shoulder pain 7 out of 10. Starting manual therapy and therapeutic exercise.
possible_clinical_impressions: Shoulder pain / possible range of motion limitation
possible_diagnoses: Shoulder pain / possible range of motion limitation

cpt_suggestions.label: therapeutic_exercise
cpt_suggestions.descriptor: Application of therapeutic procedures to one or more areas, each 15 minutes, utilizing structured exercises designed to improve, manage, or restore physiological range of motion, physical strength, muscle endurance, and soft tissue flexibility.
documentation_requirements: Objective baseline metrics for strength (e.g., manual muscle testing grades) and range of motion measured via goniometry.
Clear descriptions of specific exercises performed, including sets, repetitions, resistance levels, and anatomical areas targeted.
Explicit documentation of skilled clinical intervention, such as verbal cues for form correction, manual assistance, or physiological monitoring.
Direct correlation between the selected exercises and the functional progression goals outlined in the patient care plan.

billing_caveats.time_system: CMS 8-Minute Rule compliant. Total cumulative timed minutes across all direct treatments determine total billable units per session.

payer_specific_rules.medicare_part_b: Requires direct, one-on-one visual and manual contact by a licensed clinician or certified assistant.
payer_specific_rules.commercial_payers: Some non-Medicare lines follow the Substantial Portion Method (15-minute midpoint rules) rather than cumulative rounding.

ncci_conflict_risks: Bundled with 97140 (Manual Therapy) when executed on identical anatomical zones unless distinct time intervals and separate goals are validated.
Subject to overutilization audits if billed consistently as the sole intervention across multi-week cycles without functional progression.

cpt_suggestions.reason: Indicated when a patient presents with demonstrable deficits in joint mobility, active or passive range of motion, muscular weakness, or diminished structural endurance resulting from injury, surgery, or chronic degenerative disease processes.

cpt_suggestions.label: manual_therapy
cpt_suggestions.descriptor: Manual skilled procedures administered to one or more body regions, encompassing manual traction, joint mobilization, manual manipulation, manual lymphatic drainage, and specific soft tissue mobilization.
documentation_requirements: Clear diagnostic localized mapping of tissue restrictions, joint tracking impairments, or structural hypomobility grades.
Naming of precise anatomical segments treated (e.g., right glenohumeral joint, lumbar spine segments L4-L5).
Explicit specification of mobilization grades applied (Grades I through V) or specialized tissue stroke maneuvers used.
Immediate pre- and post-treatment reassessment of passive range of motion or tissue tension metrics to document physical efficacy.

billing_caveats.time_system: Governed by the standard CMS 8-Minute Rule parameters.

payer_specific_rules.medicare_part_b: Mandatorily requires manual, hands-on touch throughout the code runtime. Mechanical alternatives do not qualify.
payer_specific_rules.commercial_payers: Strict cap constraints often exist regarding total lifetime or annual manual therapy interventions per policy year.

ncci_conflict_risks: Triggers automatic NCCI PTP denials when combined with 97110 or 97530 on identical body regions. Requires Modifier 59 / XS only if executed on independent anatomical regions or distinct operational intervals.
Fully encompasses localized massage techniques; billing 97124 concurrently on the same region is forbidden.

cpt_suggestions.reason: Indicated when objective physical examination confirms structural joint hypomobility, restricted capsular patterns, localized myofascial trigger points, chronic muscular guarding, or post-surgical localized soft tissue adhesions.

ncci_conflicts.explanation: NCCI PTP edit: 97140 is bundled into 97110. Bypassing requires application to separate, distinct anatomical sites or documentation of entirely independent purposes.

soap_update.subjective: Patient reports shoulder pain. Pain scale: 7/10.
soap_update.objective: Manual therapy: manual therapy. Therapeutic exercise: therapeutic exercise.
soap_update.assessment: Possible clinical impressions to review: Shoulder pain / possible range of motion limitation.
soap_update.plan: Review therapy plan, skilled minutes, and documentation support for detected treatment themes. Documentation gaps: Baseline manual muscle testing (MMT) was not documented before therapeutic exercise.; Suggested fix: document baseline MMT grades for involved muscles before skilled exercise..

billing_hints: Documented 'therapeutic exercise' may support Therapeutic Exercise (97110).
Documented 'manual therapy' may support Manual Therapy Techniques (97140).
latest_analysis.disclaimer: Rules-based detection requires clinician review before billing.

assistant_suggestions.title: Document Shoulder Pain Character and Location
assistant_suggestions.body:
Patient reports 7/10 pain; consider documenting pain quality (sharp/dull/aching), exact location on shoulder, and what aggravates or relieves it to establish baseline for treatment response.

AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.
assistant_suggestions.disclaimer: AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.

assistant_suggestions.title: Capture Manual Therapy Technique Details
assistant_suggestions.body:
Document which manual therapy technique(s) were applied (e.g., soft tissue mobilization, joint mobilization), anatomical target, duration, and patient response during the intervention.

AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.
assistant_suggestions.disclaimer: AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.

assistant_suggestions.title: Specify Therapeutic Exercise Parameters
assistant_suggestions.body:
Record the specific exercises prescribed, number of sets/reps, resistance level, and any modifications made based on the patient's 7/10 pain level.

AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.
assistant_suggestions.disclaimer: AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.
